description: A supporting axis of a plant is its 'pakewu'. It bears leaves, flowers, fruit, or branches and carries material between them. Its form can be soft or woody, upright or trailing; some stems run underground. 'shiro pakewu' names a tree trunk, while 'loremi' (branch) grows from a stem or another branch.
articulatory_notes: The lips meet briefly for unvoiced 'p' and release into open 'a'. A closure at the soft palate begins stressed 'ke'; rounded 'w' then carries voice into high-back 'u'.

semantic_domains.nature: the supporting plant axis that bears leaves and reproductive structures
semantic_domains.physical: a plant structure that supports growth and carries material
